title: anthyllis vulneraria var. weldeniana
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria var. weldeniana, also known as the 'Kidney Vetch', is a subspecies of the common kidney vetch plant, characterized by its perennial nature and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Kidney Vetch include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Kidney Vetch is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in supplements and remedies.
